summary.intent: Add an embedded xterm.js terminal to the Gossamer Electron app so Claude Code can be run directly inside the app, with support for multiple persistent terminal sessions.
summary.outcome: A fully functional multi-tab terminal system was implemented using node-pty (main process), xterm.js (renderer), and a context API (TerminalContext) that allows any screen to open a named terminal instance. All sessions persist across navigation via always-mounted panels with CSS show/hide.

repo: The app uses Electron with electron-vite, React Router, and Mantine UI components.
IPC between main and renderer is bridged via contextBridge in the preload script, exposing APIs on window.pty.
Navigation is handled by React Router with an <Outlet /> in Layout.tsx — Layout is the right place to host always-mounted UI like terminals.
node-pty must be rebuilt for Electron's Node.js runtime via electron-rebuild after installation.

code.finding: fitAddon.fit() must only be called when the container is visible and has real dimensions. Initializing xterm while the container is hidden (display:none) causes the PTY to start with zero dimensions and immediately exit. Deferred initialization on first active=true resolves this.
Always-mounted terminal panels use CSS display:none/flex toggling instead of React mount/unmount to preserve PTY session state across navigation.
openTerminal({ id, cwd, label }) deduplicates by id — calling with an existing id focuses the tab rather than spawning a duplicate, enabling one-terminal-per-session patterns.
A /terminal route rendering null must exist to prevent React Router from throwing 'No routes matched location /terminal', even though the terminal UI is managed entirely by Layout.

workflow: node-pty requires a native rebuild step after npm install when used in Electron; this must be done before attempting a build.
When progressively editing Layout.tsx across many small edits, it's easy to lose track of open/close tag balance — reading the file state before edits helps avoid JSX structure bugs.

friction: Multiple iterative edits to Layout.tsx were needed to correctly structure the JSX provider wrapping, indicating the complexity of retrofitting context providers into an existing layout component.
The 'process exited' bug required a non-obvious root cause diagnosis: xterm/PTY initialization while the container was hidden with display:none caused zero-dimension sizing.
open_items: Wire up openTerminal() calls from other screens (e.g. checkpoint or session screens) to launch terminals scoped to specific repos or Claude Code sessions.
Consider adding a close/kill button per terminal tab to clean up PTY processes when sessions are no longer needed.
No tests exist for the terminal IPC layer or the TerminalContext — these could be fragile across Electron version upgrades.
